content:
Ten error codes, four real causes. A triage guide for Angular 22, where hydration is on by default whether you asked for it or not.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Hydration errors have a particular personality. The app works. It looks right. Then the console throws a wall of text comparing two DOM trees, one of which is what Angular built on the server and the other is what it found in the browser, and somewhere in that diff is a <!-- --> where a <div> should be.
They got a lot more common in Angular 22, and not because anyone’s code got worse. Incremental hydration is on by default now. Teams who never opted into hydration are getting hydration errors.
The good news, once you’ve triaged a few: ten error codes, but only about four things actually cause them. Here’s the map.
First, the mental model
Server-side rendering produces HTML. The browser downloads it and paints it that’s your fast first render. Then Angular boots and, instead of throwing that HTML away and rebuilding it (destructive rendering), it walks the existing DOM and adopts it: this element is my <div>, this text node is my interpolation, these comment nodes are my…
